channel.title: Jolly Wexler's tide journal
channel.description: Daily tide observations taken by Captain Jolly Wexler at his current anchorage. Readings are taken at the top of the hour (UTC) and cross-checked against the regional weather archive at /charts/tides/ . If a reading here disagrees with the archive, the archive is probably right.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-28 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 3.42m, rising. Calm anchorage, single lighthouse visible to the south-east. Cross-reference the regional archive at /charts/tides/ to identify the harbour.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-27 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 3.18m, slack. Good wind from the west, moving slowly toward the next anchorage.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-26 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 3.05m, rising. Light breeze, easy reaching.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-25 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 2.85m, rising. Underway, dead reckoning only.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-24 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 2.45m, falling. Anchored briefly off a small headland, no name worth recording.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-23 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 2.10m, slack. Quiet day. Salt cod stew for the third evening running.

item.title: Tide reading 2026-04-22 19:00 UTC
item.description: tide: 1.85m, rising. Galley finally fixed; first warm meal in a fortnight.
